title: Method of producing and deploying orthodontic brackets or attachments
abstract: The present invention relates to methods of producing and deploying orthodontic brackets (2a) or attachments.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of producing one or more orthodontic brackets (2a) or attachments (2b), the method comprising a) creating, by a CAM method, a dentition model (la, lb) comprising one or more tooth models (3), which model has, on its labial and/or palatal surface, at least one bracket (2a) or attachment (2b) connected on its tooth-facing side to the tooth surface via at least one linker structure (4), which linker structure is embodied as a predetermined breaking point. 2. The method according to claim 1, further comprising b) releasing the one or more brackets or attachments from the tooth surface by breaking, cleaving or dissolving the linker structure. 3. A method for transferring one or more orthodontic brackets (2a) or attachments (2b) from a dentition model, to which they are connected by linker structure, into a transfer tray, the method comprising, after step a) and before step b) of claim 2, a step al .1) in which a transfer tray comprising a negative model (5) of the dentition model is created by forming or molding a matrix material over the dentition model including the one or more brackets or attachments, thereby forming cavities (6) which accomodate the brackets or attachments, or al .2) in which transfer tray comprising a prefabricated negative model (5) of the dentition model, which negative model comprises cavities (6) to accommodate the bracket or attachment, is arranged on the dentition model. 4. The method according to claim 3, wherein, after step al.l) or al.2.), a step a2) is provided in which the transfer tray is removed from the dentition model, whereby the one or more brackets or attachments are released from the tooth surface by breaking, cleaving or dissolving the linker structure, a3) in such way that they remain in the cavities formed in the negative model. 5. A method of transferring one or more orthodontic brackets (2a) or orthodontic attachments (2b) from a prefilled transfer tray as produced according to claims 3 and/or 4 to one or more teeth of a patient, said method comprising the steps of c) arranging the transfer tray on one or more teeth of a patient, and d) attaching the one or more brackets or attachments to the one or more tooth surfaces. 6. The method according to any one of claims 3 - 5, wherein the transfer is formed by vacuum forming of a thermoplastic sheet over the dentition model. 7. The method according to any of the above mentioned claims, wherein the dentition model further comprises a model of a patientâs gum or jaw ridge (7), or of a fragment thereof, which supports the one or more tooth models. 8. A method of producing a prefilled transfer tray for the deployment of one or more orthodontic brackets or orthodontic attachments on one or more teeth of a patient, which method comprising steps a), al), a2) a3) and b) as set forth in the aforementioned claims. 9. A transfer tray for the deployment of one or more orthodontic brackets or orthodontic attachments to one or more teeth of a patient, said transfer tray comprising at least one cavity or recess that accommodates one such bracket or attachment, said transfer tray being manufactured with a method according to claim 3, step al .1) 10. A method of deploying one or more orthodontic brackets or orthodontic attachments to one or more teeth of a patient, in which method a) a transfer tray, into the cavities of which one or more orthodontic brackets or attachments have been transferred according to claim 3, is arranged over a patientâs gum or jaw ridge, or over a fragment thereof, and b) one or more orthodontic brackets or orthodontic attachments are attached to one or more labial and/or palatal surfaces of the one or more teeth. 11. A dentition model (la, lb) comprising one or more tooth models (3), which model has, on its labial and/or palatal surface, at least one bracket (2a) or attachment (2b) connected on its tooth-facing side to the tooth surface via at least one linker structure (4), which linker structure is embodied as a predetermined breaking point. 12. A transfer tray for the deployment of one or more orthodontic brackets or orthodontic attachments (12) to one or more teeth (13) of a patient, said transfer tray comprising â¢ a section (15) that is form- locking with at least a part of a crown of at least one tooth of a patient, and â¢ at least one cavity or recess (16) that accomodates or carries at least bracket or attachment (12), wherein the at least one bracket or attachment (12) is connected the tooth-facing surface of the cavity or recess (16) via at least one linker structure (14), which linker structure is embodied as a predetermined breaking point, 13. The transfer tray according to claim 12, wherein the section (15) comprises â¢ a first subsection (l5a) suitable to be releasably attached to at least a part of a crown of at least one tooth of a patient, and â¢ a second subsection (l5b) which comprises the at least one cavity or recess (16) that accomodates or carries the at least one bracket or attachment (12), wherein the two subsections are connected to one another by means of an integral hinge (17), in such way that the second subsection (l5b) can be flipped towards or away from the tooth (13). 14. The transfer tray according to any of claims 12 - 13, which transfer tray has been created with a CAM method. 15. The method according to any one of the aforementioned claims, wherein the bracket or attachment has a tooth- facing side which is form-locking with the part of the tooth it is to be attached to. 16. The method according to any of the above mentioned claims, wherein the CAM method is at least one selected from the group consisting of â¢ CNC milling â¢ Laser photolithography â¢ stereolithography â¢ 3D printing 17. The method according to any of the above mentioned claims, wherein at least one bracket or attachment comprises, on the side facing the surface of the tooth model, a microstructure (8).
